description:
A network-operations colleague has handed you a raw connection-debug log
    saved at  

        /home/user/network/logs/conn.log  

    Together with that file, an (empty) directory already exists at  

        /home/user/network/output/  

    Your job is to isolate—using pure regular-expression based CLI tools—the
    log lines that show failed connection attempts *that occurred on
    2023-09-15*, and then produce a compact, machine-readable summary of the
    distinct failure reasons.

    1. Filter the source log so that **only** the lines that satisfy *all* of
       the following conditions are kept:
       • The timestamp (field #1) is the calendar day **2023-09-15**.  
       • The “conn_status” key (field #6) has the literal value **FAIL**.  
       • The line contains exactly one “err=” key whose value is a
         three-digit numeric error code.  
       Preserve the original order of those lines.

    2. Save the resulting subset of lines, *unchanged*, to  

           /home/user/network/output/failures_2023-09-15.log  

       The automated test will read that file verbatim, so the line endings
       and ordering must stay identical to the source.

    3. From the same subset of lines, build a short ASCII report that lists,
       one per line, the count of each distinct error code sorted
       descending by count, then ascending by the numeric code for ties.
       The report must respect the exact format:  
          <error_code><single_space><count>  
       For example, a possible line might be  
          104 7  
       Place the report in  

           /home/user/network/output/error_summary.txt  

       Only those two columns—code and count—should appear; no header or
       extra spacing.

    4. All steps must be done *exclusively* with shell tools that support
       regular expressions (grep, egrep, awk, sed, perl, etc.).  Running
       several commands in parallel pipelines is fine.

    NOTE FOR THE AUTOMATED CHECKER  
    • It will confirm that /home/user/network/output/failures_2023-09-15.log
      contains the precise expected subset (order preserved, no additional
      lines).  
    • It will verify that /home/user/network/output/error_summary.txt matches
      the exact counts and ordering rule.  
    • Make sure both files exist and are world-readable (mode 0644 is fine).

    Do *not* modify conn.log itself.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth data below is used by the test harness and must never be
    revealed in the public task description.
    -->

    <!-- Original log file that is already present before the agent starts -->
    <initial_file path="/home/user/network/logs/conn.log"><![CDATA[
2023-09-14T23:58:12Z router=sfo-gw1 src=10.0.0.2 dst=8.8.8.8 proto=ICMP conn_status=OK latency=34ms
2023-09-15T00:01:44Z router=nyc-core src=172.16.4.89 dst=1.1.1.1 proto=TCP conn_status=FAIL err=101 detail="SYN timeout"
2023-09-15T00:02:53Z router=lon-edge src=192.168.1.4 dst=8.8.4.4 proto=UDP conn_status=OK
2023-09-15T01:15:22Z router=nyc-core src=10.10.10.10 dst=142.250.72.238 proto=TCP conn_status=FAIL err=104 detail="RST received"
2023-09-15T02:47:03Z router=sfo-gw1 src=10.0.0.3 dst=198.41.0.4 proto=UDP conn_status=FAIL err=108 detail="Port unreachable"
2023-09-15T03:10:37Z router=lon-edge src=192.168.1.22 dst=8.8.8.8 proto=ICMP conn_status=FAIL err=101 detail="TTL exceeded"
2023-09-15T04:21:12Z router=nyc-core src=172.16.5.5 dst=9.9.9.9 proto=TCP conn_status=FAIL err=104 detail="RST received"
2023-09-15T04:45:44Z router=sfo-gw1 src=10.0.0.25 dst=8.8.4.4 proto=UDP conn_status=OK
2023-09-15T05:33:19Z router=lon-edge src=192.168.1.42 dst=8.8.4.4 proto=UDP conn_status=FAIL err=108 detail="Timeout"
2023-09-15T06:55:01Z router=nyc-core src=10.10.10.11 dst=142.250.72.238 proto=TCP conn_status=FAIL err=101 detail="SYN timeout"
2023-09-15T08:00:00Z router=lon-edge src=192.168.1.45 dst=8.8.8.8 proto=ICMP conn_status=OK latency=37ms
2023-09-15T09:09:09Z router=sfo-gw1 src=10.0.0.50 dst=198.41.0.4 proto=UDP conn_status=FAIL err=104 detail="Unknown"
2023-09-15T10:10:10Z router=nyc-core src=172.16.5.6 dst=1.1.1.1 proto=TCP conn_status=FAIL err=108 detail="ECN failure"
2023-09-15T11:11:11Z router=lon-edge src=192.168.1.46 dst=9.9.9.9 proto=UDP conn_status=OK
2023-09-16T00:00:00Z router=sfo-gw1 src=10.0.0.2 dst=8.8.8.8 proto=ICMP conn_status=FAIL err=104 detail="Next-day test"
    ]]></initial_file>

    <!-- Expected filtered file -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/network/output/failures_2023-09-15.log"><![CDATA[
2023-09-15T00:01:44Z router=nyc-core src=172.16.4.89 dst=1.1.1.1 proto=TCP conn_status=FAIL err=101 detail="SYN timeout"
2023-09-15T01:15:22Z router=nyc-core src=10.10.10.10 dst=142.250.72.238 proto=TCP conn_status=FAIL err=104 detail="RST received"
2023-09-15T02:47:03Z router=sfo-gw1 src=10.0.0.3 dst=198.41.0.4 proto=UDP conn_status=FAIL err=108 detail="Port unreachable"
2023-09-15T03:10:37Z router=lon-edge src=192.168.1.22 dst=8.8.8.8 proto=ICMP conn_status=FAIL err=101 detail="TTL exceeded"
2023-09-15T04:21:12Z router=nyc-core src=172.16.5.5 dst=9.9.9.9 proto=TCP conn_status=FAIL err=104 detail="RST received"
2023-09-15T05:33:19Z router=lon-edge src=192.168.1.42 dst=8.8.4.4 proto=UDP conn_status=FAIL err=108 detail="Timeout"
2023-09-15T06:55:01Z router=nyc-core src=10.10.10.11 dst=142.250.72.238 proto=TCP conn_status=FAIL err=101 detail="SYN timeout"
2023-09-15T09:09:09Z router=sfo-gw1 src=10.0.0.50 dst=198.41.0.4 proto=UDP conn_status=FAIL err=104 detail="Unknown"
2023-09-15T10:10:10Z router=nyc-core src=172.16.5.6 dst=1.1.1.1 proto=TCP conn_status=FAIL err=108 detail="ECN failure"
    ]]></expected_file>

    <!-- Expected summary file -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/network/output/error_summary.txt"><![CDATA[
101 3
104 3
108 3
    ]]></expected_file>

    <!-- File modes that the grading harness will verify -->
    <file_mode path="/home/user/network/output/failures_2023-09-15.log" mode="0644"/>
    <file_mode path="/home/user/network/output/error_summary.txt" mode="0644"/>
